parsedJson.summary: The site uses a dated, cluttered layout with a low-quality stock photo and hidden contact information, making it difficult for a local customer to trust the business or take action.

issues.title: Contact details hidden in tiny grey text
issues.what_observed: The phone number (07 3889 6688) and address are located in a small, light-grey font at the very top of the page, sandwiched between a teal banner and the logo.
issues.why_problem: A local customer searching for a roofer wants to call immediately. If they have to squint or hunt for the number, they will assume the business is inactive or unprofessional and click back to Google.
issues.what_correct_looks_like: A large, bold phone number (e.g., 24px+) in the top right corner or a sticky 'Call Now' button on mobile that is visible without scrolling.
issues.how_to_fix_in_redesign: Move the phone number to the top-right of the header in a large, bold, clickable font. On mobile, implement a sticky bottom bar with a 'Call' button.

issues.title: Stock photo looks fake and irrelevant
issues.what_observed: The main hero image features a family pointing upwards with exaggerated facial expressions, overlaid with a heavy blue tint.
issues.why_problem: The image looks like a generic stock photo from 2010. It does not show actual roof renovations, which fails to prove the business has the capability to do the job. It feels 'salesy' rather than professional.
issues.what_correct_looks_like: A high-quality, real photograph of a completed attic renovation or a roof space conversion, showing the actual work the company does.
issues.how_to_fix_in_redesign: Replace the stock photo with a real project shot of a finished roof space. If real photos aren't available, use a clean architectural render, but remove the 'pointing family' overlay.

issues.title: Too many menu items create visual noise
issues.what_observed: The navigation bar contains 8 distinct links (Home, About Us, Our Services, Our Product Range, Be Inspired, Our Showroom, FAQs, Contact Us) crammed into a single line.
issues.why_problem: This creates 'choice paralysis.' A visitor doesn't know where to click first. It also makes the header look crowded and unorganized, suggesting the business itself is disorganized.
issues.what_correct_looks_like: A simplified menu with 4-5 key items (e.g., Services, Projects, About, Contact). Secondary items like FAQs or Showroom should be moved to the footer or a dropdown.
issues.how_to_fix_in_redesign: Reduce the top menu to 4 items: Services, Projects, About, Contact. Move 'FAQs', 'Showroom', and 'Product Range' to the footer or a 'More' dropdown.

issues.title: Call-to-action button is hard to find
issues.what_observed: The primary action button ('Get in touch for a free quote!') is a yellow rectangle buried at the bottom of the hero section, below a large paragraph of text.
issues.why_problem: Users scan pages; they don't read. By hiding the button below a wall of text, you force the user to read everything before they can act. Most will leave before reaching the button.
issues.what_correct_looks_like: A prominent button placed immediately next to the headline or in the top navigation bar, using a high-contrast color (like orange or red) to stand out against the white/blue background.
issues.how_to_fix_in_redesign: Move the 'Get a Free Quote' button to the top right of the navigation bar. Add a second, larger button directly under the main headline in the hero section.

issues.title: Font choices feel old-fashioned
issues.what_observed: The logo uses a serif font with a drop shadow, and the body text uses a standard sans-serif that lacks hierarchy.
issues.why_problem: The typography feels like it belongs to a business website from 2012. It doesn't convey the precision and modernity expected of a construction/renovation company.
issues.what_correct_looks_like: Clean, bold sans-serif fonts (like Montserrat or Roboto) for headings to convey strength, and a highly readable font for body text.
issues.how_to_fix_in_redesign: Update the logo font to a cleaner, bolder sans-serif. Increase the font size of the body text to 16px or 18px for better readability.

positive_observations: The value proposition 'Same House, More Home' is clear and easy to understand.
The color scheme (Teal/Yellow/White) is generally cohesive and readable.
The headline 'Bigger. Brighter. Better.' effectively summarizes the benefit of the service.
redesign_priorities: 1. Make the phone number and 'Get a Quote' button immediately visible and large.
2. Replace the stock photo with real project imagery to build trust.
3. Simplify the navigation menu to reduce clutter and focus the user.
